_comment: Example aictl MCP servers config. Copy to ~/.aictl/mcp.json (or point AICTL_MCP_CONFIG at this path) and set AICTL_MCP_ENABLED=true in ~/.aictl/config to opt in. Each entry under 'mcpServers' is { command, args, env, enabled, timeout_secs }. 'command' is resolved via PATH (no shell); 'env' values support a ${keyring:NAME} substitution that pulls a secret from the system keyring (or plain config). Edit the absolute path under 'tiny_add' to match where you cloned aictl.

tiny_add._comment: Minimal smoke-test server bundled with aictl. Exposes one tool (mcp__tiny_add__add). Replace the absolute path with the location on your machine.

filesystem._comment: Official Anthropic MCP server, fetched on demand via npx. The trailing arg is the directory the server is allowed to access — keep it scoped tightly.

github._comment: Official GitHub MCP server in a Docker container. Set GITHUB_TOKEN in the system keyring (e.g. via /keys) and the ${keyring:GITHUB_TOKEN} placeholder is resolved at startup — the literal token is not stored in this file.
